question:
Will 100 Thieves win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.
event_title:

evidences.title: 100 Thieves qualifies for LoL Worlds 2024
evidences.content: With that victory, the 100 Thieves LCS team also qualified for LCS Finals weekend. Depending on the results of those matches, 100 Thieves will make their Worlds appearances in either the Play-Ins, or the Swiss Stage. Additionally, this marks 100 Thieves’ 4th Worlds appearance in its LCS history. Watch 100 Thieves play against Flyquest in the lower bracket finals this Friday September 5th. Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. [...] In the do-or-die match against Cloud9, 100 Thieves pulled out a tremendous upset victory against the North American superteam, eliminating analyst-favorites Cloud9 and punching their own tickets to Europe. Worlds 2024 will be the debut international event appearance for all but River on the team. Beginning the season with aspirations of just getting a rookie team up to speed, Sniper, River, Quid, Tomo, and Eyla along with coaching staff GoldenGlue, Spookz, and Kaimera have surpassed all expectations. [...] Checkout ## Your cart is empty! 100 Thieves qualifies for LoL Worlds 2024 ALL # 100 Thieves qualifies for LoL Worlds 2024 ### 100 Thieves League of Legends LCS Team qualifies for LoL Worlds 2024, defeating Cloud 9 in a best-of-five ma...

evidences.title: The LoL Global Contract Database (GCD) - RFT.GG
evidences.content: T1 Worlds 2025 Photo Credit: Colin Young-Wolff/Riot Games The Global Contract Database, commonly known as the GCD, is Riot Games' official registry of every player and coach currently signed to a team in the worldwide League of Legends competitive circuit. It is the single source of truth for who plays for whom — and until when. ### What the GCD Contains The database lists, for each registered individual: The player or staff member's in-game name and legal name Their current team and role Their contract end date Their residency status (a critical detail for import rules in regions like the LEC or LCS) A team contact point for transfer inquiries [...] LEC Summer Finals are heading to Nice - LoL Esports Chris Greeley: "There's never been a better time to be a League esports fan" - Team Heretics sOAZ joins Team Heretics as positional coach 1 - SK Gaming SlowQ officially joins SK Gaming for the LEC Summer Split 3 - MSI MSI 2026 Main Event Day 4 — The challengers push, the favourites answer - FURIA FURIA Tatu: "The next step for Brazil is to do well at internationals" - Esports World Cup France Télévisions to Broadcast the 2026 Esports World Cup in Paris 2 The LoL Global Contract Dat...

evidences.title: Introducing League of Legends Championship of The Americas
evidences.content: Equipo invitado provisional 100 Thieves: fundado en 2017, 100 Thieves dejó su marca rápidamente alcanzando la final de la LCS en su primer año y ganando su primer campeonato de la LCS en 2021, consolidándose como uno de los principales contendientes. En 2025, 100 Thieves será parte de la liga como invitado provisional. 100 Thieves decidió vender su plaza de franquicia a Riot Games por motivos de negocio y Riot decidió comprar la plaza de nuevo y crear un plan de transición mientras lanzamos la LTA. En la temporada 2025, 100 Thieves competirá con pleno acceso a la LTA y a los torneos internacionales, pero no podrá participar en el Torneo de Ascenso a finales de 2025. Revisaremos nuestros planes para la plaza de equipo invitado provisional durante 2025. Equipo invitado [...] Lyon Gaming: Six Karma y Movistar R7, dos competidores de la LLA, se unen para traer de regreso una marca icónica en la historia de Latinoamérica como parte de la conferencia norte en 2025, como el socio oficial de Latinoamérica. Cloud9: Cloud9 fue el primer equipo norteamericano desde la temporada 1 en alcanzar las semifinales en Worlds, logrando este hito histórico en 2018 y solidificando su posición en la h...

evidences.title: Everything YOU Need to Know About 100T for Worlds 2025 - YouTube
evidences.content: We already know that 100 Thieves is out for 2026 because we know Sentinels is replacing them. So, for the players and coaches on this team, this year was really their last shot with the ORC to make something happen. Here's the team card. You might notice something funky about it. Yes, 100 Thieves had to play eight best of fives to qualify to Worlds. The reason why the number is so ridiculously high is because 100 Thieves actually spent a bulk of the split as the literal worst team in LTA North. They finished regular season without a single series win and there were players quoted as saying 100 Thieves was the easiest opponent to beat. During the stretch of time, they were playing the same roster as last year in the prior two splits with Sniper, a young mechanical talent from NA. Sniper's [...] Hey guys, Porro here with another Worlds 2025 team breakdown this time for 100 Thieves from the LTA. For the people who haven't seen Split 3 or who only loosely followed the LTA as a whole, you might be surprised to see the Thieves here instead of more established teams like TL or Z9. For what it's worth, I was just as surprised as you all are now when I was watching through their regular...

evidences.title: Worlds 2025: Meet All the Teams Competing for the Title
evidences.content: Qualifying for Worlds is already an incredible leap for the team and a showcase not only for the entire LTA South region but also for the global scene — proving that with hard work, a team can grow tremendously. For Keyd Stars, finishing in the Top 10–8 would already be a great result, serving as a springboard for the years to come. ### 100 Thieves Another team from LTA North, 100 Thieves delivered fairly consistent results throughout the 2025 season and rightfully earned their spot at Worlds 2025. Unfortunately, they didn’t get the chance to compete internationally earlier this year to build momentum before Worlds, but within their region, their performance has been very solid. evidences.title: All teams qualified for LoL Worlds 2025 - esports.gg
evidences.content: ### LTA FlyQuest Vivo Keyd Stars 100 Thieves FlyQuest has been dominating the LTA scenario since 2024, with a roster full of talent. Once again, they were superior in the LTA North, getting a pass to the Cross Conference, where they defeated Vivo Keyd Stars. The Brazilian team qualified as the second seed, followed by 100 Thieves. FlyQuest qualified as the first seed to the LoL Worlds 2025 FlyQuest defeated VKS in the LTA 2025 Championship (Image via Stefan Wisnoski | Riot Games) ### LEC G2 Esports Movistar KOI FNATIC [...] Gen.G won both the MSI and the LCK 2025 Season Playoffs Gen.G won both the MSI and the LCK 2025 Season Playoffs (Image via Riot Games) ### LPL Bilibili Gaming Anyone’s Legend Top Esports IG (needs to defeat T1 to qualify) In the LPL 2025 Grand Finals, BLG defeated Top Esports 3-2, becoming the champions of the Split. Anyone’s Legend qualified for the tournament due to their Championship Points, and also for being the third seed of the region. Now, their fourth seed will have to face LCK’s fourth seed for an opportunity to play in the Play-Ins Stage. T1 versus Invictus Gaming will happen on Oct. 14, 2025, at 4 p.m. CST / 10 a.m. CET / 1 a.m. PT. BLG, AL and TE...

evidences.title: An Echo of Immortals in the 2018 Spring Split - The Game Haus
evidences.content: And Immortals’ time in the NA LCS ended there. They would not get a new opportunity to dominate North America like 2016, or go to Worlds like 2017. The team fully disbanded, and the league moved on. ## EX-IMMORTALS IN 2018 Immortals traded Dardoch to CLG in 2017 Image from LoL Esports Flickr Following Riot’s permanent partners announcement, Team Liquid acquired most of Immortals’ released roster. Xmithie, Pobelter, Cody Sun, Anda and Ollehjoined the organization initially, but Cody Sun went on to 100 Thieves and Anda went to FlyQuest. Flame and Stunt signed with FlyQuest, as well. TSM picked up Coach Ssong to lead their new roster. [...] Four fifths of Immortals’ 2017 roster met in the last stage of playoffs. Xmithie, Pobelter, Olleh and Cody Sun made it to the finals, yet again, with Team Liquid winning the whole split and 100 Thieves second. Flame, Anda and Stunt finished the split in eighth place, and Coach Ssong finished fifth-sixth with TSM. However, this was the first time Anda and Stunt entered a split as starters. Flame performed perfectly fine as an individual top laner. And Coach Ssong helped build TSM into a formidable team, even if they fell short in playoffs. [...]...
